316.play: Pale as thy smock! when we shall meet at compt,

317.play: This look of thine will hurl my soul from heaven,

318.play: And fiends will snatch at it. Cold, cold, my girl!

319.play: Even like thy chastity. O cursed slave!

321.play: From the possession of this heavenly sight!

322.play: Blow me about in winds! roast me in sulphur!

323.play: Wash me in steep-down gulfs of liquid fire!

326.play: LODOVICO: Where is this rash and most unfortunate man?

327.play: OTHELLO: That's he that was Othello: here I am.

328.play: LODOVICO: Where is that viper? bring the villain forth.

329.play: OTHELLO: I look down towards his feet; but that's a fable.

330.play: If that thou best a devil, I cannot kill thee.

331.play: LODOVICO: Wrench his sword from him.

332.play: IAGO: I bleed, sir; but not kill'd.

333.play: OTHELLO: I am not sorry neither: I'ld have thee live;

334.play: For, in my sense, 'tis happiness to die.

335.play: LODOVICO: O thou Othello, thou wert once so good,

336.play: Fall'n in the practise of a damned slave,

337.play: What shall be said to thee?

339.play: An honourable murderer, if you will;

340.play: For nought I did in hate, but all in honour.

341.play: LODOVICO: This wretch hath part confess'd his villany:

342.play: Did you and he consent in Cassio's death?

344.play: CASSIO: Dear general, I never gave you cause.

345.play: OTHELLO: I do believe it, and I ask your pardon.

346.play: Will you, I pray, demand that demi-devil

347.play: Why he hath thus ensnared my soul and body?

348.play: IAGO: Demand me nothing: what you know, you know:

349.play: From this time forth I never will speak word.

351.play: GRATIANO: Torments will ope your lips.

353.play: LODOVICO: Sir, you shall understand what hath befall'n,

354.play: Which, as I think, you know not. Here is a letter

355.play: Found in the pocket of the slain Roderigo;

356.play: And here another: the one of them imports

357.play: The death of Cassio to be undertook

360.play: CASSIO: Most heathenish and most gross!

361.play: LODOVICO: Now here's another discontented paper,

362.play: Found in his pocket too; and this, it seems,

363.play: Roderigo meant to have sent this damned villain;

364.play: But that belike Iago in the interim

367.play: How came you, Cassio, by that handkerchief

369.play: CASSIO: I found it in my chamber:

370.play: And he himself confess'd but even now

371.play: That there he dropp'd it for a special purpose

374.play: CASSIO: There is besides in Roderigo's letter,

375.play: How he upbraids Iago, that he made him

376.play: Brave me upon the watch; whereon it came

377.play: That I was cast: and even but now he spake,

378.play: After long seeming dead, Iago hurt him,

380.play: LODOVICO: You must forsake this room, and go with us:

381.play: Your power and your command is taken off,

382.play: And Cassio rules in Cyprus. For this slave,

383.play: If there be any cunning cruelty

384.play: That can torment him much and hold him long,

385.play: It shall be his. You shall close prisoner rest,

386.play: Till that the nature of your fault be known

387.play: To the Venetian state. Come, bring him away.

388.play: OTHELLO: Soft you; a word or two before you go.

389.play: I have done the state some service, and they know't.

390.play: No more of that. I pray you, in your letters,

391.play: When you shall these unlucky deeds relate,

392.play: Speak of me as I am; nothing extenuate,

393.play: Nor set down aught in malice: then must you speak

394.play: Of one that loved not wisely but too well;

395.play: Of one not easily jealous, but being wrought

396.play: Perplex'd in the extreme; of one whose hand,

397.play: Like the base Indian, threw a pearl away

398.play: Richer than all his tribe; of one whose subdued eyes,

399.play: Albeit unused to the melting mood,

400.play: Drop tears as fast as the Arabian trees

401.play: Their medicinal gum. Set you down this;

402.play: And say besides, that in Aleppo once,

403.play: Where a malignant and a turban'd Turk

404.play: Beat a Venetian and traduced the state,

405.play: I took by the throat the circumcised dog,

408.play: GRATIANO: All that's spoke is marr'd.

409.play: OTHELLO: I kiss'd thee ere I kill'd thee: no way but this;

410.play: Killing myself, to die upon a kiss.

411.play: CASSIO: This did I fear, but thought he had no weapon;

412.play: For he was great of heart.

414.play: More fell than anguish, hunger, or the sea!

415.play: Look on the tragic loading of this bed;

416.play: This is thy work: the object poisons sight;

417.play: Let it be hid. Gratiano, keep the house,

418.play: And seize upon the fortunes of the Moor,

419.play: For they succeed on you. To you, lord governor,

420.play: Remains the censure of this hellish villain;

421.play: The time, the place, the torture: O, enforce it!

422.play: Myself will straight aboard: and to the state

423.play: This heavy act with heavy heart relate.
